description_long: This is a medieval-style battle axe with a long, straight handle and a large, single-bladed head. The handle appears to be made of dark wood or metal, cylindrical in shape, and extends to a pointed metal tip at the top. The axe head is broad and crescent-shaped, with a sharp cutting edge and a pronounced beard, while the opposite side features a hammer-like poll. The blade is mounted perpendicular to the handle, and the mounting appears robust and secure. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative engravings or embellishments. The color palette is primarily dark, with black and metallic tones dominating the appearance.
description: A long-handled, dark-colored battle axe with a broad crescent blade and a hammer poll, featuring a robust, straight handle.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the front, showing the narrow profile of the blade and the pointed tip of the handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the side, clearly displaying the crescent-shaped blade and the hammer poll opposite the blade.
description_view_2: The rear view shows the narrow profile of the blade and the handle, similar to the front but from the opposite side.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals the full shape of the blade and the hammer poll, mirroring view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical handle, likely wood or metal, smooth surface, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad crescent-shaped blade with a sharp edge and pronounced beard, opposite a hammer poll, no visible decorations.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted perpendicular to the handle.
